issues.title: SCurve SVG missing accessible keyboard navigation and ARIA live region for hovered data
issues.description: The SVG chart uses onKeyDown for ArrowLeft/ArrowRight but has no visible focus indicator, no aria-live region announcing the hovered data point value, and data point circles lack individual aria-labels. Screen reader users get no feedback when navigating the chart.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/budget/SCurve.tsx, in the SCurve component: Add a visually hidden aria-live='polite' div below the SVG that announces the currently selected data point (month and value) when `hovered` state changes. BEFORE: The outer div has tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown but no focus style and no aria-live announcement. The SVG data point circles (if rendered) have no aria-label. AFTER: 1) Add `outline: '2px solid ' + colors.primaryOrange` and `outlineOffset: '2px'` to the outer div's focus style via onFocus/onBlur state or CSS-in-JS focus selector. 2) Add a div after the SVG with style `{position:'absolute',width:1,height:1,overflow:'hidden',clip:'rect(0,0,0,0)'}` and `aria-live='polite'`. When `hovered` changes to a non-null index, set its textContent to `${months[hovered]}: Actual $${actualData[hovered]}M, Planned $${plannedData[hovered]}M`. 3) Add `role='listitem'` and `aria-label={months[i] + ': $' + actualData[i] + 'M'}` to each actual data point circle element in the SVG. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: getPayApplications silently returns empty array on permission errors masking 403s
issues.description: The getPayApplications function catches errors and throws, but assertProjectAccess may throw a generic Error. If the Supabase query returns a 403 due to RLS, the error message is raw Supabase text. No user-friendly error mapping exists for pay app fetches, and the caller cannot distinguish 'no data' from 'no permission'.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts, in the getPayApplications function: BEFORE: The function calls `assertProjectAccess(projectId)` then does `if (error) throw error` which throws the raw Supabase PostgrestError object. The caller has no way to distinguish an empty result (no pay apps yet) from a permission denial. AFTER: 1) After the Supabase query, check `if (error)` and use `transformSupabaseError(error)` (already imported) to throw a user-friendly error. 2) If `error.code === '42501'` or the message includes 'permission' or 'policy', throw `new Error('You do not have permission to view payment applications for this project.')`. 3) For other errors, throw `transformSupabaseError(error)`. This ensures the UI can show 'You do not have permission' for 403s vs a helpful empty state for genuinely empty data. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: WaterfallChart mobile bars lack touch targets and have tiny text unreadable in field
issues.description: On mobile, bar labels are 90px wide with xs fontSize. The bar height is only 28px, below the 44px Apple HIG touch target minimum. A superintendent viewing the budget waterfall on their iPhone cannot tap individual bars to see values. The hover tooltip has no touch equivalent.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/budget/WaterfallChart.tsx, in the mobile rendering branch (the `isMobile ? (` section): BEFORE: Each bar container has `minHeight: 48` but the actual colored bar div inside has `height: 28`. Label width is 90px with `fontSize: typography.fontSize.xs`. There is no onClick handler on the bar, only onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave. AFTER: 1) Change the bar div height from 28 to 44 to meet Apple HIG touch target minimum. 2) Change the label fontSize from `typography.fontSize.xs` to `typography.fontSize.sm`. 3) Add an `onClick` handler on each bar div that sets `setHoveredBar(bar.label)` (toggle: if already hovered, set null). This gives touch users a tap-to-reveal interaction equivalent to hover. 4) Update `minHeight` on the row container from 48 to 56 to accommodate the taller bar. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: computeCurrentPaymentDue does not validate that currentPctComplete >= prevPctComplete
issues.description: The AIA G702 payment calculation allows currentPctComplete < prevPctComplete which would produce a negative workThisPeriod. In construction billing, percent complete can only go forward within a pay period. A PM accidentally entering 40% when previous was 60% would generate a negative payment due, which is financially invalid and could cause accounting errors.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts, in the computeCurrentPaymentDue function: BEFORE: The function accepts any values for prevPctComplete and currentPctComplete without validation. If currentPctComplete < prevPctComplete, workThisPeriod becomes negative, producing an incorrect negative payment. There is no guard or warning. AFTER: 1) At the top of the function body, add validation: `if (currentPctComplete < prevPctComplete) { throw new Error('Current percent complete (' + (currentPctComplete * 100).toFixed(1) + '%) cannot be less than previous percent complete (' + (prevPctComplete * 100).toFixed(1) + '%). Percent complete can only increase within a pay period.') }`. 2) Also add: `if (currentPctComplete > 1 || currentPctComplete < 0 || prevPctComplete > 1 || prevPctComplete < 0) { throw new Error('Percent complete values must be between 0 and 1 (0% to 100%).') }`. 3) Add: `if (retainageRate < 0 || retainageRate > 1) { throw new Error('Retainage rate must be between 0 and 1.') }`. This prevents financially invalid calculations from silently producing wrong numbers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: EarnedValueDashboard has no real-time subscription and shows stale EV metrics
issues.description: The EarnedValueDashboard fetches budget data via useBudgetData hook on mount but has no Supabase real-time subscription. When another PM updates budget line items or approves a change order, the EV metrics (CPI, SPI, EAC) remain stale until manual refresh. The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires real-time updates within 2 seconds for budget data.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/budget/EarnedValueDashboard.tsx, in the EarnedValueDashboard component: BEFORE: The component calls `useBudgetData()` which fetches on mount but does not subscribe to real-time changes. There is no `useEffect` with a Supabase channel subscription. When another user modifies budget_items or change_orders, the EV metrics remain stale. AFTER: 1) Import `{ supabase }` from `'../../lib/supabase'` and `{ useEffect }` if not already imported. 2) Import `useProjectContext` from `'../../stores/projectContextStore'`. 3) After the `useBudgetData()` call, get projectId: `const { currentProjectId } = useProjectContext()`. 4) Add a `useEffect` that creates a Supabase channel subscription: `const channel = supabase.channel('ev-dashboard-' + currentProjectId).on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'budget_items', filter: 'project_id=eq.' + currentProjectId }, () => { /* trigger refetch if useBudgetData exposes refetch, otherwise use a state counter to force re-render */ }).subscribe(); return () => { supabase.removeChannel(channel) };` with dependency `[currentProjectId]`. If `useBudgetData` does not expose a `refetch` function, add a `refreshKey` state counter incremented in the callback and pass it as a dependency to the hook. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's financial module lacks earned value analysis entirely and does not compute CPI/SPI. SiteSync's EarnedValueDashboard and AIA G702 computeCurrentPaymentDue function are significant differentiators. However, Procore's pay app workflow is fully real-time and has robust permission-based error messaging, while SiteSync's pay app endpoint lacks proper 403 distinction and the EV dashboard shows stale data. Sage Intacct integration (P0-1 in roadmap) is the single biggest gap preventing GC adoption since 70%+ of target customers use Sage.
summary: The financial engine scores 45 overall with strong domain depth in AIA G702 formulas and earned value computation but critical gaps in accessibility (SCurve chart is not screen reader navigable), error handling (pay app endpoint masks permission errors as empty data), mobile usability (WaterfallChart bars are too small to tap), data validation (negative percent complete produces invalid payments), and real-time updates (EV dashboard shows stale metrics). The five surgical fixes target: accessible chart navigation with aria-live announcements, distinguishing permission errors from empty state in pay apps, touch-friendly mobile bar sizing, input validation on AIA calculations to prevent financial errors, and real-time subscriptions on the earned value dashboard. These fixes address the three lowest dimensions (accessibility 28, real-time 32, mobile 35) per the plateau strategy.
